title: Gitattributes Merge with Linguist-Generated Markers
description: WHY: PRs are cluttered with machine-generated specledger artifacts. GitHub supports collapsing these via linguist-generated in .gitattributes. Current template is empty and copy logic either overwrites or skips.

title: Setup Phase
description: WHY: Template and manifest need to be updated before merge logic can be implemented.
design: Update .gitattributes template with linguist-generated patterns. Add Mergeable field to Playbook struct and FilesMerged to CopyResult. Add mergeable list to manifest.yaml.

title: Populate .gitattributes template with linguist-generated patterns
description: WHY: The current .gitattributes template contains only a comment. It needs linguist-generated patterns for issues.jsonl and tasks.md.

items.item: No other files marked as linguist-generated

design: File: pkg/embedded/templates/specledger/.gitattributes. Replace placeholder comment with two patterns: specledger/*/issues.jsonl linguist-generated=true and specledger/*/tasks.md linguist-generated=true. No sentinel markers in template - those are added at runtime.
acceptance_criteria: Template contains exactly 2 linguist-generated patterns for issues.jsonl and tasks.md. No spec.md, plan.md, checklists, or other files are marked.

title: Add Mergeable field to Playbook struct and FilesMerged to CopyResult
description: WHY: The playbook system needs to distinguish mergeable files from normal copy files.

items.item: Mergeable field added to Playbook struct
FilesMerged field added to CopyResult struct

design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/template.go. Add Mergeable []string yaml:"mergeable,omitempty" to Playbook struct after Protected. Add FilesMerged int to CopyResult struct.
acceptance_criteria: Playbook struct has Mergeable field. CopyResult has FilesMerged counter. YAML deserialization works for mergeable list.

title: Add mergeable list to manifest.yaml
description: WHY: The manifest needs to declare which files use merge semantics instead of copy.
design: File: pkg/embedded/templates/manifest.yaml. Add mergeable: [".gitattributes"] under the specledger playbook, after the protected section.
acceptance_criteria: manifest.yaml has mergeable list with .gitattributes entry. Manifest loads and parses correctly.

title: Foundational Phase
description: WHY: The sentinel merge function is a prerequisite for all user stories. It must exist before the copy flow can be wired up.
design: Create pkg/cli/playbooks/merge.go with MergeSentinelSection() pure function. Adapt pattern from pkg/cli/context/updater.go extractManualAdditions(). Use strings.Index() for sentinel detection.

title: Implement MergeSentinelSection() pure function
description: WHY: Core merge logic needed before wiring into copy flow. Must handle all sentinel states: no file, no sentinels, valid sentinels, malformed sentinels.

items.item: Handles existing content with valid sentinels (replace)
Handles malformed sentinel - begin without end (replace to EOF per FR-011)
Idempotent - same input produces same output

design: New file: pkg/cli/playbooks/merge.go. Constants: SentinelBegin = "# >>> specledger-generated", SentinelEnd = "# <<< specledger-generated". Function MergeSentinelSection(existing, managed string) string. Algorithm: 1) Build sentinel block with managed content 2) If both markers found (begin < end): replace range 3) If begin found but no end (malformed FR-011): replace from begin to EOF 4) If neither found: append block. Adapt from pkg/cli/context/updater.go extractManualAdditions() pattern using strings.Index().
acceptance_criteria: Pure function takes existing file content and managed content, returns merged result. Handles all 4 states: no file, no sentinels, valid sentinels, malformed. Idempotent.

title: Add table-driven tests for MergeSentinelSection
description: WHY: The merge function is the core of this feature. Comprehensive tests are needed to ensure correctness across all edge cases.

items.item: Test: append to existing without sentinels
Test: preserves user content outside sentinels
All tests pass with go test

design: New file: pkg/cli/playbooks/merge_test.go. Table-driven tests covering: 1) Empty existing + managed content 2) Existing without sentinels (append) 3) Existing with valid sentinels (replace, preserve surrounding) 4) Idempotency (merge twice = same result) 5) Malformed sentinel (begin without end) 6) User content before and after sentinels 7) Empty managed content 8) Existing with only whitespace.
acceptance_criteria: All test cases pass. Coverage includes all 4 sentinel states plus edge cases.

title: US1+US2: Wire merge into copy flow
description: WHY: The copy system needs to use merge instead of copy/skip for mergeable files. This enables both first-time init (US1) and re-init merge (US2).

items.item: mergeableMap built in CopyPlaybooks from playbook.Mergeable
--force flag does not overwrite mergeable files

design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/copy.go. In CopyPlaybooks(): build mergeableMap from playbook.Mergeable (same pattern as protectedMap). Pass mergeableMap to copyStructureItem(). In copyStructureItem(): check mergeableFiles[structureItem] before protected check, call mergeFile() if true. New function mergeFile(srcPath, destPath string, opts CopyOptions, result *CopyResult) error: reads template from embedded FS, reads existing file from disk (if any), calls MergeSentinelSection(), writes result. Respects DryRun. Increments FilesMerged.
acceptance_criteria: sl init creates .gitattributes with sentinel block when no file exists. sl init merges into existing .gitattributes preserving user content. sl init --force also merges (not overwrites). sl doctor --template also merges (shares same code path via CopyPlaybooks).

title: Wire mergeableMap into CopyPlaybooks and copyStructureItem
description: WHY: The copy orchestration needs to know which files are mergeable to route them to mergeFile instead of copySingleFile.

items.item: Mergeable check happens before protected check

design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/copy.go. In CopyPlaybooks() after building protectedMap: build mergeableMap from playbook.Mergeable. Update copyStructureItem signature to accept mergeableFiles map[string]bool. At top of copyStructureItem, before protected check: if mergeableFiles[structureItem] { return mergeFile(srcPath, destPath, opts, result) }.
acceptance_criteria: Mergeable files are routed to mergeFile. Non-mergeable files follow existing copy logic unchanged. Protected and overwrite logic is bypassed for mergeable files.

title: Implement mergeFile function in copy.go
description: WHY: Mergeable files need different handling than copySingleFile - they must read existing content, merge via MergeSentinelSection, and write the result.
design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/copy.go. New function: func mergeFile(srcPath, destPath string, opts CopyOptions, result *CopyResult) error. Steps: 1) Read template from embedded FS via ReadFile(srcPath) 2) Read existing file from disk via os.ReadFile(destPath) - nil if not exists 3) Call MergeSentinelSection(existing, template) 4) Create parent dirs 5) If !opts.DryRun: write merged content with 0644 perms 6) Increment result.FilesMerged.
acceptance_criteria: mergeFile reads embedded template and existing file, merges via MergeSentinelSection, writes result. Handles missing destination file. Respects DryRun. Increments FilesMerged.

title: Update ApplyToProject reporting for merged files
description: WHY: Users need feedback that files were merged (not just copied/skipped) during init and doctor operations.
design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/templates.go. In ApplyToProject(), after the FilesCopied and FilesSkipped reporting: add if result.FilesMerged > 0 { fmt.Printf("Merged %d file(s)\n", result.FilesMerged) }.
acceptance_criteria: When files are merged, user sees Merged N file(s) in output. When no files merged, no extra output.

title: US3: Idempotent Re-runs Verification
description: WHY: Need to verify that repeated sl init produces byte-identical .gitattributes. This is inherent to the MergeSentinelSection design but needs explicit verification.

title: Add idempotency test case to merge_test.go
description: WHY: SC-003 requires running sl init N times produces byte-identical output. Need explicit test that merge(merge(x)) == merge(x).

items.item: Idempotency test case added and passes

design: File: pkg/cli/playbooks/merge_test.go. Add test case: call MergeSentinelSection twice with same input, assert outputs are identical. Already part of the foundational test suite but needs explicit named test case for traceability.
acceptance_criteria: Test explicitly verifies merge(merge(existing, managed), managed) == merge(existing, managed).

title: Polish and Cross-Cutting Concerns
description: WHY: Final verification, build check, and manual testing across all user stories.

title: Run go build and full test suite
description: WHY: Ensure all changes compile and existing tests still pass.
design: Run: go build ./cmd/sl/ and go test ./pkg/cli/playbooks/... -v
acceptance_criteria: Build succeeds. All existing tests pass. New merge tests pass.

title: Manual end-to-end testing per quickstart.md
description: WHY: Automated tests cover the merge function but manual testing verifies the full sl init flow.

items.item: New repo: .gitattributes created with sentinel block
Existing repo: user content preserved, sentinel block appended

design: Follow specledger/609-gitattributes-merge/quickstart.md testing section. Test: 1) sl init in new repo 2) sl init in repo with existing .gitattributes 3) sl init repeated 3x for idempotency 4) sl init --force with existing .gitattributes.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 manual test scenarios pass per quickstart.md.

title: Integration Tests: sl init and sl doctor gitattributes merge
description: WHY: Unit tests cover MergeSentinelSection but we need integration tests that build the sl binary, set up fixture directories with pre-existing .gitattributes, and test both sl init and sl doctor --template flows end-to-end.
design: New file: tests/integration/gitattributes_test.go. Follow existing patterns from bootstrap_test.go and doctor_test.go: t.TempDir() for isolation, buildSLBinary() for compilation, os/exec.Command() for execution. Tests run in parallel via t.Parallel(). Optionally add initProject() helper to test_helper.go.

title: Test: sl init creates .gitattributes in new project
description: WHY: Validates US1 — first-time init populates .gitattributes with sentinel block.
design: File: tests/integration/gitattributes_test.go. Function: TestGitattributesInitCreatesFile. Steps: build binary, create empty project dir, run sl init --short-code test --ci, read .gitattributes, assert contains sentinel markers and both linguist-generated patterns.
acceptance_criteria: .gitattributes created with sentinel begin/end markers and issues.jsonl + tasks.md patterns.

title: Test: sl init merges into existing .gitattributes
description: WHY: Validates US2 — existing user content is preserved when sentinel block is appended.
design: File: tests/integration/gitattributes_test.go. Function: TestGitattributesInitMergesExisting. Steps: build binary, create project dir, write .gitattributes with user content (*.pbxproj binary, *.png binary), run sl init --short-code test --ci, read .gitattributes, assert user content preserved AND sentinel block present.
acceptance_criteria: User content preserved. Sentinel block appended. Both patterns present.

title: Test: sl init updates existing sentinel block
description: WHY: Validates US2 — when sentinels already exist, only the managed section is replaced.
design: File: tests/integration/gitattributes_test.go. Function: TestGitattributesInitUpdatesSentinel. Steps: build binary, create project dir, write .gitattributes with user content + old sentinel block (only issues.jsonl), run sl init, assert user content preserved, sentinel block now contains both patterns, only ONE begin marker (no duplication).
acceptance_criteria: Old sentinel content replaced with new. User content preserved. No duplicate markers.

title: Test: sl init is idempotent
description: WHY: Validates US3/SC-003 — repeated sl init produces byte-identical .gitattributes.
design: File: tests/integration/gitattributes_test.go. Function: TestGitattributesInitIdempotent. Steps: build binary, create project with user content in .gitattributes, run sl init twice, compare file content after each run, assert byte-identical.
acceptance_criteria: Two consecutive sl init runs produce identical .gitattributes content.

title: Test: sl init --force merges (not overwrites)
description: WHY: Validates FR-010 — force flag must not overwrite mergeable files.
design: File: tests/integration/gitattributes_test.go. Function: TestGitattributesInitForceMerges. Steps: build binary, create project dir, write .gitattributes with user content, run sl init --force --short-code test --ci, assert user content preserved and sentinel block present.
acceptance_criteria: User content preserved even with --force. Sentinel block present.

title: Test: sl init handles malformed sentinel (begin without end)
description: WHY: Validates FR-011 — malformed sentinel is auto-fixed by replacing from begin to EOF.
design: File: tests/integration/gitattributes_test.go. Function: TestGitattributesInitMalformedSentinel. Steps: build binary, create project dir, write .gitattributes with user content + begin marker + old content but NO end marker, run sl init, assert user content before begin marker is preserved, malformed content replaced with correct sentinel block, exactly one begin AND one end marker.
acceptance_criteria: User content before begin marker preserved. Content from begin to EOF replaced. Exactly one begin and one end marker.

title: Test: sl doctor --template merges .gitattributes
description: WHY: Validates that sl doctor --template uses the same merge path as sl init, preserving user content.
design: File: tests/integration/gitattributes_test.go. Function: TestGitattributesDoctorTemplateMerges. Steps: build binary, run sl init to create project, manually add user content outside sentinels in .gitattributes, run sl doctor --template from project dir, assert user content preserved and sentinel block updated.
acceptance_criteria: sl doctor --template preserves user content and updates sentinel block.

title: Test: sl doctor --template is idempotent
description: WHY: Validates that repeated sl doctor --template produces identical .gitattributes.
design: File: tests/integration/gitattributes_test.go. Function: TestGitattributesDoctorTemplateIdempotent. Steps: build binary, init project, run sl doctor --template twice, compare .gitattributes after each run, assert byte-identical.
acceptance_criteria: Two consecutive sl doctor --template runs produce identical .gitattributes.

title: Expand quickstart.md with sl doctor UX
description: WHY: Quickstart only documented sl init path, not sl doctor --template. Users need to know both entry points.
design: File: specledger/609-gitattributes-merge/quickstart.md. Add sl doctor --template usage section with examples. Add integration test commands to Development section.
acceptance_criteria: Quickstart documents both sl init and sl doctor --template flows.
